name: Garlic Soup
recipeIngredient: or half of stock and water
1 Ounce each of freshly grated Parmesan and Swiss cheese
4 toasted bread croutons made of sliced, optional French bread
1 tablespoon chopped parsley for garnish (1 to 2)
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring stock to a boil in a nonreactive saucepan (do not use aluminum as it will react with eggs later).
2. Add bay leaf, sage, thyme, and garlic cloves; cook, covered, at a bare simmer for 30 minutes.
3. Strain broth and discard herbs; press garlic through to liquid and season to taste with salt.
4. In a mixing bowl, combine cheese with egg, yolk, olive oil, and pepper to taste (preparation can be done in advance).
5. Just before serving, temper the eggs by drizzling a ladleful of hot soup into the egg mixture; pour this tempered mixture back into the soup.
6. Whisk continuously over low heat until the soup thickens slightly. Avoid overheating to prevent curdling.
7. Serve on a plate with a piece of French bread crouton, garnishing with parsley.
Yield: 4 servings
